pageDescription: A collection of small and quirky web games
domainWar_description: A choice-driven text adventure about the kakao.games domain dispute
kakao_disclaimer: This site is an independent project, not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Kakao Corp. or Kakao Games Corp. Kakao is a trademark of Kakao Corp.
welcomeMessage:
Welcome!

This is kakao.games.
There's only one game here so far. 😅

Everything has to start somewhere.
Small, weird little games will keep showing up
one at a time.

Right now you can play 'Domain War,'
also known as 'The Adventure of Speling Artisan.'

⚔️ A narrative text RPG about a real domain dispute!

Your choices branch the story, shift its mood,
and might even tug at your heart a little.

I made all of this.
I'm Speling Artisan.

I can't spell to save my life,
but I mean every word.
domainCh1_title: ⚔️ Domain War: The Adventure of Speling Artisan — Chapter 1
domainCh1_scene0_text:
🌍 Spring, 2025.

You're brainstorming a mini-game called "kakao"
when a domain search turns up something interesting.

🔎 'kakao.games' — available. Just $30.

It's fate.

You buy it on the spot and dive into building
the site, buzzing with excitement.

Then, a few days later,
📩 a warning email lands from the legal team at K Corp,
a company several sizes bigger than you.

"The domain you registered may cause confusion with
our registered trademark, and we are reviewing whether
this constitutes an infringement of our rights."

You take a deep breath.
Then you have to decide.
domainCh1_scene0_choice0_text: ⚙️ Ignore it and continue game development
domainCh1_scene1_text:
⚙️ You decide to keep building,
operating on a simple "ship first, worry later" policy.

Rhythm games, pixel-art RPGs...
your head is overflowing with ideas.

But K Corp moves faster than you expected.
A few days later, you get notified of a UDRP
(Uniform Domain-Name Dispute-Resolution Policy) complaint.
domainCh1_scene2_text:
💼 You email your lawyer friend.

A little later, your phone rings.

📞 "Okay, this is kind of funny,
but it happens more than you'd think.

Domain disputes really come down to one thing,
whether you registered it in bad faith.

Since you were actually trying to build a game,
you've got a solid defense."

That makes you feel a lot better.
But it's too soon to relax.

K Corp was already gearing up to file the UDRP complaint.
domainCh1_scene3_text:
🛡️ The warning email is still open when your hands
move on their own. You log into the familiar comfort
of Cloudflare and throw the shield up.

Having done something, anything, you let yourself
breathe for a moment.
domainCh1_scene4_text:
🧙‍♂️ Your mind drifts to a creature of legend.
The Vercel Dragon.

A name passed around in whispers among developers.
They say once you call it, there's no taking it back,
but this is no time to worry about that.

You steady yourself and decide to summon it.
Far off, a low rumble begins to roll in...
domainCh1_scene5_text:
🚀 Launch day is right around the corner.
You hope everything's ready, but time is
running short. Somewhere between anxious and excited,
you dive into one last round of checks.
Will the launch go off without a hitch?
domainCh2_title: 🛡️ Domain War: The Adventure of Speling Artisan — Chapter 2
domainCh2_scene0_text:
⚖️ Barely after you rushed the game out the door,
K Corp files a formal UDRP complaint with WIPO,
almost like they'd been waiting for the chance.

📑 The filing reads:

"The domain 'kakao.games' causes confusion with our
brand and game division, and the respondent clearly
intends to gain unfair profit through this domain."

📞 Your lawyer calls. "Oh boy… this one's for real."

Rushing the launch came back to bite you.
Time to get your defenses up.
domainCh2_scene1_text:
🛡️ The cloud shield is up, but the relief
doesn't last long.

It hits you: this was never a traffic problem.
You brought an umbrella to a swordfight.
K Corp isn't attacking your servers, they're
coming at you with lawyers.

The WIPO panel sends a notice:
📩 "Submit your counter-arguments within 20 days."

Speling Artisan's fingers hover over the keyboard...
domainCh2_scene1_choice0_text: 📄 Write a rebuttal on the legitimate purpose of use
domainCh2_scene1_choice1_text: 💰 Offer to sell K Corp the domain at a steep markup
domainCh2_scene2_text:
🧙‍♂️ You log into the Vercel console,
connect your GitHub repo, and hit 'Deploy.'

💥 The sky splits open, and a dragon descends
in a column of light.

"Your deployment... is ready."

The Vercel Dragon speaks.
"Prove that this project is real. That will
be your shield."
domainCh2_scene2_choice0_text: 🕹️ Create and release a game demo page
domainCh2_scene3_text:
📄 Your fingers dance across the keyboard, crafting
a careful rebuttal that lays out why this domain
matters and what you're really building.

But a quiet unease creeps in.
The moment is coming when words won't be enough,
when you'll have to show something real.
domainCh2_scene4_text:
💰 You email K Corp, offering to sell them the domain
for a hefty sum.

The reply comes fast.

📩 "Thank you for the offer. Trying to resell the domain
to the trademark holder is precisely the 'bad-faith
registration' we've been arguing, now in writing."

Oh no.
You just handed them their best piece of evidence.

This can't stand.
You'll have to turn it around with something real,
not words.
domainCh2_scene5_text:
🕹️ You build a demo of 'Domain War'
and release it on kakao.games.

Satisfied, the dragon certifies your project as
the real thing.

"You are not just a squatter... you are a creator."

Those words will carry more weight now
than any rebuttal could.
domainCh2_scene6_text:
🔒 You switch the domain to private
and quietly step away.

And just like that, kakao.games
seems to fade into history.

But hiding was never going to end it.
The verdict is still coming, and something
pulls you back to your feet.
domainCh3_title: 🐉 Domain War: The Adventure of Speling Artisan — Chapter 3
domainCh3_scene0_text:
🐉 As the verdict draws near,
the voice of the Vercel Dragon rings out from somewhere.

"Deployment is only the beginning… a creation with
no substance has no claim to justice."

It tells you to build up three things.

1️⃣ Meta info and a favicon
2️⃣ Real, usable content
3️⃣ A reaction from the community

"Build them up, one by one.
With each, your claim grows stronger…"

Where do you start?
domainCh3_scene0_choice0_text: 🧠 Set up metatags and favicon first
domainCh3_scene1_text:
🧠 You immediately set up the basic SEO groundwork,
<meta name="description">, a favicon, an og:image.

The dragon nods.
"Trust is earned by respecting the fundamentals."
domainCh3_scene2_text:
🎮 You officially deploy the 'Domain War' demo
at kakao.games/games/kakao-adventure.

People can actually play it, click through it,
make choices in it. It's real.

The Vercel Dragon mutters,
"That is the mark of true creation…"
domainCh3_scene3_text:
🌐 You post on Instagram with hashtags:
#kakaogames #indiegame #thisistherealdomainwar

You drop it on a humor board too, just to see what happens.

🔥 The community starts to react.

- "Wait, you actually picked a fight with K Corp? lol"
- "the vibe of this game is unhinged, I love it"

As the voices pile up,
the dragon slowly nods.

"Where people gather, substance takes root.
You are the true owner of kakao.games."
domainCh3_scene4_text:
📩 Everything you've got goes to the UDRP panel.
Now you'll be judged as a legitimate creator, not
just a domain squatter.

The real war starts here.
domainCh4_title: ⚖️ Domain War: The Adventure of Speling Artisan — Chapter 4
domainCh4_scene0_text:
📩 Your rebuttal is in with the UDRP. Every piece
of evidence, every explanation, all of it, ready.
Now there's nothing left to do but wait.

A few days later, an email arrives.
The subject line is one sentence long.

📥 "Decision Regarding kakao.games"

You open it with trembling fingers.

Applicant's claim, the domain causes confusion
with their trademark.
Respondent's claim, legitimate use for real content.

The UDRP panel will announce its ruling soon.
domainCh4_scene1_text:
💼 The verdict is in.

"While the domain 'kakao.games' may cause some
confusion, the respondent is using it legitimately,
and the evidence of bad-faith registration is insufficient."

Which means,
✅ Decision: the domain stays with the respondent.

You won!
kakao.games proudly stays in your name.
domainCh4_scene2_text:
🕯️ Sipping a warm cup of drip coffee,
you read the message one more time.

"The domain is not subject to transfer."

A small smile crosses your face. You think of
the Vercel Dragon.

"Now the real adventure begins."
domainCh4_scene3_text:
🎮 You've just won your first battle
in the Domain War.

But this isn't the end. It's the starting line
for a much bigger story.

So what do you fill kakao.games with now?

That question is the whole next chapter.
domainCh4_scene4_text:
🔁 You go back to the start,
take a different path this time,
and chase down another ending…
domainCh5_title: 🎮 Domain War: The Adventure of Speling Artisan — Chapter 5
domainCh5_scene0_text:
🏆 You won.
Legally, technically, and every other way that counts.

Whatever anyone says, kakao.games is a domain
that exists under your name now.

But the real question starts here.

What games will you fill it with? What kind of vibe,
what kind of approach, and with who?
domainCh5_scene0_choice1_text: 👾 Decide to collect strange, experimental works
domainCh5_scene0_choice2_text: 🧩 Officially release 'Domain War' itself as a game
domainCh5_scene1_text:
🎨 You pull out a big sketchbook and dig up
that music game you half-built years ago, the pixel
platformer, the puzzle games that never got past a hackathon
whiteboard…

You decide to pour every last one of those ideas
into kakao.games.

This isn't just a domain anymore.
It's a stage for your whole world.
domainCh5_scene2_text:
👾 You start collecting things like 'a game that ends
the moment you press one button,' 'an RPG you play with
a single line of text,' 'a conversation sim with no
background at all.'

Strange, mostly pointless little experiments.

Before long, kakao.games becomes a sanctuary for
developers and a home for bizarre ideas.
domainCh5_scene3_text:
🧩 You decide to release the whole chronicle of
'Domain War' as an actual game.

Story branches, choices, dragons, Cloudflare…
your real experience becomes a genuinely gripping story.

kakao.games becomes the flagship title for
reality-based interactive fiction.
domainCh5_scene4_text:
🌱 You open your project folder and
create a file called `games/001-first-game.tsx`.

Then you type the first line.

"This is a very small,
and maybe quirky, game."

Right then,

💡 the real story of kakao.games begins.
domainCh5_scene5_text:
🚀 You release your very first game to the world.
kakao.games isn't a symbol of conflict anymore.

It's a home base for making things.
It's Speling Artisan's little universe.

And… another challenge is already waiting for you.
domainCh6_title: 🌌 Domain War: The Adventure of Speling Artisan — Chapter 6
domainCh6_scene0_text:
📈 After your first game goes live,
kakao.games slowly starts picking up buzz.

On Reddit, Twitter, Discord,
people trade takes like "weird, but I kind of love it."

Then one day,
someone slides into your DMs.

"Can I upload my game here too?"
domainCh6_scene0_choice1_text: 🧵 Operate on a selective, invitation-only basis
domainCh6_scene0_choice2_text: 🧙‍♂️ No, this is my personal laboratory
domainCh6_scene1_text:
🙌 With a simple "Sure, go for it,"
you write up a solid README covering the commit
guidelines, the story rules, and how deployment works.

What used to be a quiet little alley slowly turns
into a playground for indie developers.
domainCh6_scene2_text:
🧵 You lay down the law.
"This place is for strange games only."

You only invite in games with a certain
'peculiar sensibility.'

Somehow, all these rare, unfamiliar, bizarre games
end up fitting together perfectly.

And that's exactly what people love about it.
domainCh6_scene3_text:
🧙‍♂️ You say, flatly,
"I made this place for myself."

Every week, you make and upload one more game
built around one tiny idea.

People start showing up every week just to see
what's new. That's all it takes.
domainCh6_scene4_text:
📮 One day, an email like this shows up.
"Dear Speling Artisan, would my game be a good fit
for kakao.games?"

Another day, it's a DM.
"I genuinely love this place. It doesn't even feel
like a game, more like reading someone's diary."

You quietly close the tab
and open your text editor back up.
domainCh6_scene5_text:
🌌 People don't really remember kakao.games for
the 'Domain War' anymore. They remember it as
'Speling Artisan's Little Universe.'

Here, people play more seriously than anywhere else,
more weirdly than anywhere else,

each in their own particular way.

And that story is far from over.
domainCh7_title: 🧥 Domain War: The Adventure of Speling Artisan — Chapter 7
domainCh7_scene0_text:
🧥 One day,
a website that's somehow both familiar and
not catches your eye.

https://kakagames.io

You click through… The design looks similar,
and the tagline feels oddly familiar too.

"A collection of small and quirky web games."

A thought drifts through your head.
'Wait… isn't this my site?'
domainCh7_scene0_choice2_text: 💣 Send a formal protest email to the site
domainCh7_scene1_text:
🧠 You just laugh it off.
"Eh, as long as it's fun, who cares."

And you call it a night.
That night, you have a strange dream.

Every domain in the world, slowly turning
into 'kakao.something.'

You wake up and think,
'What have I actually unleashed on the world…?'
domainCh7_scene2_text:
🕵️ You trace the domain registration and dig
through the GitHub source.

Oh…
there's a familiar-looking username in the commit log.

"spelingbot"

And sitting right there in the README, one line.

"This site was deeply inspired by kakao.games."
domainCh7_scene3_text:
💣 You send a polite but firm email.

"Your site's similarity to kakao.games is confusing
users, and I'd appreciate it if you could either change
the design or add clear attribution.

I get the compliment, but it's causing real confusion."

A few hours later, a reply lands.

"Honestly, your whole world was just so good I wanted
to copy it… I'm really sorry. I'll fix it right away."
domainCh7_scene4_text:
🌌 You open kakao.games and just sit with
the title for a moment.

Some people copy your work.
Some people are genuinely moved by it.
Some just lift it wholesale into their own space.

This isn't a website anymore.
It's a whole culture.

And you're standing right at the start of it.
domainCh8_title: 👑 Domain War: The Adventure of Speling Artisan — Chapter 8
domainCh8_scene0_text:
📰 "kakao.games: The Website That Only Hosts
Weird Games"

A post that started on some random community blog
spreads through news sites, Twitter, and newsletters,
reaching hundreds of thousands of people in a single day.

After that, your inbox catches fire.

"Could you speak about this at our conference?"
"We want to redesign our brand in the style
of kakao.games!"
"Would you be up for a small offline exhibition
with us?"
domainCh8_scene1_text:
🎤 You stand on the conference stage.
The host introduces you.

"Today, we're introducing someone who changed
the world with a single domain name."

You take the mic.
"I mean… I just defended a $30 domain I bought."
domainCh8_scene2_text:
🖼️ You rent a small corner gallery and put up
screenshots of the site, scraps of source code,
fan letters, and player reviews.

Right at the end hangs one small framed line.

"These games were too small to be anything
but words."
domainCh8_scene3_text:
📫 You don't reply to any of it.

Instead, like any other day,
you make one more small game
and upload it to kakao.games.

It's called 'Silence in the Noise.'

And you leave it at that.
domainCh8_scene4_text:
🌌 kakao.games isn't just a domain anymore.
People talk about it like a legend.

And the person who made it is still, even now,
quietly opening up one more small page.

The next page is sitting right in front of you.
domainCh9_title: 🌀 Domain War: The Adventure of Speling Artisan — Finale
domainCh9_scene0_text:
🌒 One day, some ordinary developer types a single
word into a domain search bar.

"makao.games"

🔎 Available

They think, 'Maybe this is destiny…'

Somewhere far away, at that exact moment,
someone smiles, just barely.

The smile feels strangely familiar.
domainCh9_scene1_text:
🎮 kakao.games is still open, right where it's
always been.

Speling Artisan is still out there somewhere,
opening a text file, building a one-button game,
carefully tucking away some new feeling.

Someone sees it and smiles. Somewhere else,
someone else gets an idea and starts searching
for a domain of their own.
domainCh9_scene2_text:
🔁 You go back to the beginning of the story.

Maybe this time you make different choices,
set a different tone.

Or maybe… a whole new war is about to start.
